title: buckleya henryi
intro: Buckleya henryi, also known as the Henry's buckleya or Chinese angelica, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the subtropical forests of China and Vietnam. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The leaves and roots of Buckleya henryi are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and have been found to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Buckleya henryi can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and has also been used in traditional Chinese medicine as a component of various formulations and prescriptions.
